frameworks.name: continuity field / serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: temporal averaging of recent percepts stabilizes current perception

frameworks.name: motion priming
frameworks.one_line: prior motion biases subsequent motion perception positively

core_question: Does the perception-stabilizing continuity field also apply to dynamic attributes like motion direction, or does it reverse to enhance change sensitivity?
key_assumptions: serial dependence reflects exploitation of short-term scene correlations
stable vs dynamic attributes might recruit opposite dependency signs
manipulations: interleaved stimulus combinations mixing motion and orientation history
motion direction discrimination task with varying preceding feature
key_findings: observed a cubic-like pattern of dependencies with positive and negative components
isolated a positive (attractive) perceptual dependency for motion direction
isolated a negative (repulsive) perceptual dependency for orientation
weighted linear sum of separate dependencies reproduced the cubic pattern
interpretations: attractive and repulsive serial dependencies sum linearly across features
the stabilizing continuity field operates pervasively, even on dynamic motion stimuli
